title: Renewable Energy Integration
description: What are the main technical, economic, and regulatory challenges for integrating large‑scale renewable energy into national power grids, and what policy solutions have proven effective?
narrative: The user is looking for recent (e.g., last five years) scholarly articles, government reports, or case studies that analyze barriers such as grid stability, market design, storage needs, and regulatory frameworks, and that propose concrete policy measures or best‑practice recommendations. Relevant material must discuss at least one of these challenge areas and present evidence‑based solutions or policy recommendations.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: What are the main drivers and mitigation strategies for urban heat islands in midsize cities?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive information on the causes, magnitude, and alleviation of urban heat island (UHI) effects specifically in midsize (population 100,000‑500,000) cities. Relevant documents should discuss climatological, morphological, and socioeconomic factors that intensify UHIs (e.g., surface albedo, building density, lack of vegetation, anthropogenic heat), present quantitative measurements or modeling results, and evaluate mitigation measures such as green roofs, urban forestry, cool pavements, or policy interventions. Preference is given to peer‑reviewed journal articles, conference papers, or government reports published within the last ten years that include case studies, comparative analyses, or meta‑analyses. Documents that only mention UHIs in passing, focus on megacities, or lack empirical evidence are not considered relevant.

title: Hiring AI Bias
description: What strategies and tools can organizations use to detect and mitigate bias in AI-driven hiring systems?
narrative: The user wants a thorough overview of how to identify, evaluate, and reduce bias in automated recruitment processes. Relevant material should cover technical methods for bias detection (e.g., fairness metrics, model audits), mitigation techniques (e.g., re‑weighting, adversarial debiasing, post‑processing), real‑world case studies, industry best‑practice guidelines, ethical and legal considerations, and actionable steps that companies can take to ensure fair hiring outcomes.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do urban heat island effects vary across different city morphologies and what mitigation strategies are most effective?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly, governmental, or technical sources that analyze how the intensity of the urban heat island phenomenon changes with variations in city layout, building density, surface materials, and green space. Relevant documents should provide empirical data, simulations, or case studies that quantify temperature differences and evaluate mitigation approaches such as increased vegetation, cool roofs, reflective pavements, and urban planning interventions. Materials that only discuss unrelated topics (e.g., recipes, product ads) are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Mitigation
description: What strategies and technologies can cities implement to reduce the urban heat island effect during summer months?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on measures that municipal planners and policymakers can adopt to lower ambient temperatures in densely built environments. Relevant content should describe passive design approaches (e.g., reflective surfaces, green roofs, tree canopy expansion), active cooling technologies (e.g., district cooling, misting systems), policy instruments (e.g., building codes, incentives), and case studies demonstrating effectiveness. Documents focusing solely on personal sun protection accessories or unrelated product listings are not relevant.

title: Offshore Wind Financing
description: What are the main financing mechanisms and recent trends for developing offshore wind farms in Europe?
narrative: The user wants detailed, up‑to‑date information on how offshore wind projects in Europe are funded, including debt, equity, green bonds, public‑private partnerships, subsidy schemes, and risk‑sharing arrangements. Relevant sources should cover financing structures, key investors, policy incentives, and recent (last five years) case studies of European offshore wind developments. Documents that only address on‑shore wind, non‑European projects, or unrelated energy sectors do not satisfy the need.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the key components of effective renewable energy policies in European Union member states?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed information on legislative, economic, and regulatory measures that define successful renewable energy policies across EU countries. Relevant material should describe specific policy instruments (e.g., feed‑in tariffs, renewable portfolio standards, tax incentives), implementation frameworks, and illustrative case studies or evaluations that demonstrate outcomes, preferably covering the period from 2015 onward.

title: Offshore Wind Policy
description: What recent (2023‑2024) policy measures have European nations implemented to accelerate offshore wind energy development?
narrative: The user wants authoritative sources that describe specific legislative, regulatory, or financial initiatives introduced by European governments between 2023 and 2024 to support offshore wind projects. Relevant documents should detail the type of measure (e.g., subsidies, tax incentives, permitting reforms, grid integration plans), the target capacity or timeline, and the expected impact on renewable energy goals. Materials that only discuss offshore wind technology without linking it to a concrete policy action, or that refer to older policies (pre‑2023), are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Wellness
description: What are the mental health effects of prolonged remote work and what strategies can mitigate them?
narrative: The user wants recent, evidence‑based information on how sustained remote work impacts employee mental health—covering issues such as stress, isolation, burnout, and anxiety—and practical interventions or best‑practice recommendations to address these problems. Relevant results should include empirical studies, systematic reviews, or expert guidelines published after 2019 that discuss causes, prevalence, and effective mitigation strategies.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the key policy incentives that have driven renewable energy adoption in Europe since 2010?
narrative: The user seeks detailed information on European governmental and EU-level policies—such as feed‑in tariffs, tax credits, renewable portfolio standards, and the 2009/28/EC Renewable Energy Directive—that have significantly increased renewable electricity capacity. Relevant documents must describe specific measures, their implementation dates, and quantitative impacts on wind, solar, and bioenergy deployment. Materials focusing solely on commercial banking, insurance client portals, or unrelated corporate services are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Island
description: What mitigation strategies are most effective for reducing urban heat island effects in mid‑size cities?
narrative: The user is seeking targeted information on practical interventions (e.g., cool roofs, street trees, reflective pavements, water features) that have demonstrably lowered ambient temperatures in urban areas with populations between 100,000 and 500,000. Relevant documents should include quantitative results, cost‑benefit analyses, implementation guidelines, or case‑study evaluations. Materials that only discuss general climate change, large‑metropolitan strategies, or theoretical models without empirical outcomes are not relevant.

title: Tuber Crop IPM
description: What integrated pest management strategies are most effective for minimizing disease and pest losses in early- and late-maturing tuber crops such as potatoes and yacon?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive, evidence‑based recommendations for integrated pest management (IPM) that address both fungal diseases (e.g., downy mildew, wart) and nematode infestations across tuber varieties with differing maturation periods. Relevant documents should discuss cultural, biological, and chemical controls, treatment timing relative to crop phenology, and comparisons of efficacy between early‑ and late‑maturing cultivars. Materials focusing solely on marketing, sales, or generic gardening advice that do not provide detailed IPM tactics are not relevant.

title: Battery Recycling
description: What are the most effective current methods for recycling lithium‑ion batteries and how do they compare in terms of environmental impact and material recovery rates?
narrative: The user is seeking comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on the processes used to recycle lithium‑ion batteries, including mechanical, pyrometallurgical, hydrometallurgical, and emerging direct‑recycling techniques. Relevant documents should describe each method's steps, the proportion of battery materials (e.g., lithium, cobalt, nickel, manganese) that can be recovered, energy consumption, emissions, and any economic or regulatory considerations. Sources that only mention general waste management or non‑lithium‑ion battery recycling are not relevant.

title: Moon Mission Sustainability
description: What are the technical and economic challenges of establishing a long‑term human presence on the lunar surface?
narrative: The user is looking for detailed, up‑to‑date information on how to create and maintain a permanent or semi‑permanent human settlement on the Moon. Relevant material should cover engineering solutions (habitat design, life‑support systems, energy generation), in‑situ resource utilization (e.g., water extraction, regolith processing), launch and logistics costs, budgetary analyses, and any current or near‑future program plans (NASA Artemis, ESA lunar initiatives, private sector concepts). Documents that focus only on historic Apollo missions, fictional portrayals, or unrelated planetary bodies are not relevant.

title: Offshore Wind Policy
description: What policy measures have European countries implemented to support offshore wind energy development between 2015 and 2023?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative information on legislative and regulatory actions, financial incentives, permitting processes, and grid‑integration strategies specifically aimed at offshore wind projects in European nations during the period 2015‑2023. Relevant documents should detail concrete policies, implementation timelines, stakeholder roles, and any reported outcomes or impact assessments. Sources that merely mention offshore wind in passing, discuss unrelated renewables, or provide purely editorial opinions without policy specifics are not relevant.

title: Carbon Pricing Impact
description: How does carbon pricing affect manufacturing competitiveness in the European Union?
narrative: The user is looking for analytical resources—such as academic papers, policy reports, or detailed industry studies—that examine the economic consequences of carbon pricing mechanisms (e.g., the EU Emissions Trading System) on the cost structures, export performance, and investment decisions of manufacturing firms within the EU. Relevant content must provide empirical evidence, model results, or case‑based evaluations that link carbon pricing to manufacturing competitiveness, include quantitative metrics or sector‑specific insights, and discuss any mitigation or adaptation strategies employed by firms. Sources that merely describe environmental outcomes, focus on non‑EU regions, or address carbon pricing without a clear connection to manufacturing competitiveness are considered non‑relevant.

title: Quantum Error Correction
description: What are the most effective quantum error‑correction codes for near‑term superconducting qubits?
narrative: The user is seeking up‑to‑date, technical information on quantum error‑correction schemes that have been demonstrated or evaluated on superconducting quantum processors. Relevant documents should describe specific codes (e.g., surface codes, cat codes, bosonic codes), report on their logical error rates, qubit overhead, and fault‑tolerance thresholds, and cite experimental or simulation results from peer‑reviewed sources published between 2018 and 2024. Materials that only discuss abstract theory without connection to superconducting hardware, or that focus on unrelated platforms (trapped ions, photonics) are not relevant.

title: Urban Heat Island
description: What mitigation strategies are most effective for reducing urban heat island effects in densely built cities?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence‑based interventions—such as green roofs, cool pavements, urban forestry, and reflective building materials—that have been shown to lower ambient temperatures in high‑density urban environments. Relevant sources should provide quantitative results (e.g., temperature reductions), discuss implementation costs or feasibility, and ideally include case studies, municipal reports, or peer‑reviewed research. Documents that focus solely on rural areas, purely theoretical models without empirical data, or unrelated climate change topics are not considered relevant.

title: Fiber Decommissioning Impact
description: What are the socioeconomic and technical impacts of decommissioning copper telephone networks in France over the next decade?
narrative: The user seeks an in‑depth analysis of France's transition from copper to fiber broadband, focusing on the consequences of copper network retirement. Relevant documents should discuss the timeline and scale of copper decommissioning, migration strategies for residential and business customers, regulatory or policy measures governing the transition, cost implications for operators and end‑users, and broader socioeconomic effects such as digital inclusion, rural connectivity, and market competition. Sources that provide case studies, expert assessments, or official statements about these aspects are considered relevant; documents merely mentioning events, hotels, or unrelated logistics are not.

title: 14th Century Food Preservation
description: How were perishable foods stored and preserved in European households during the 14th century?
narrative: The user is seeking historical information on methods used to keep food edible in the 1300s, such as salting, smoking, drying, pickling, and storage in cellars or ice houses. Relevant documents should describe the techniques, the types of foods affected, regional variations across Europe, and cite primary medieval sources or reputable secondary scholarship on medieval diet and technology.

title: Solar Incentives 2024
description: What federal tax credits and state rebates are currently available for residential solar panel installations in the United States?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive, up‑to‑date information on financial incentives for homeowners installing solar panels, including the federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC), state‑level rebates or tax credits, eligibility requirements, monetary amounts, application procedures, and any recent changes or expiration dates. Relevant documents should detail these programs, their geographic scope, and practical steps for claiming them.

title: Urban Heat Islands
description: How do socioeconomic factors influence the intensity and distribution of urban heat islands in major US cities?
narrative: The user is seeking research studies, reports, or data analyses that examine the relationship between socioeconomic variables (such as income level, housing density, and access to green space) and the severity of urban heat islands across large metropolitan areas in the United States. Relevant documents should provide quantitative measurements of temperature differentials, discuss methodological approaches (e.g., satellite thermal imaging, ground sensor networks), and explicitly link those findings to socioeconomic indicators. Materials that focus solely on climate mechanics without social context, or that address heat islands in non‑US locations, are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the most effective policy mechanisms for accelerating renewable energy adoption in developing economies?
narrative: The user wants detailed, evidence‑based information on incentives, subsidies, regulatory frameworks, and case studies that have successfully increased renewable energy deployment in low‑income countries. Relevant sources should include scholarly analyses, government reports, or reputable NGOs that evaluate and compare policy outcomes, offering concrete recommendations. Generic overviews, commercial product listings, or unrelated travel information are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policies
description: What government policies have proven most effective at accelerating renewable energy adoption in developing countries?
narrative: The user seeks a detailed overview of policy instruments (e.g., feed‑in tariffs, tax incentives, regulatory mandates, financing mechanisms) that have demonstrably increased renewable electricity capacity in low‑ and middle‑income nations. Relevant documents should describe specific policies, provide evidence of impact (e.g., capacity added, investment attracted), discuss cost and scalability, and address implementation challenges. Sources that only mention general renewable energy concepts without concrete policy analysis are not relevant.

title: Low‑Glycemic Sweeteners
description: What are the best natural low‑glycemic sweeteners for baking, and how do they compare in terms of blood‑sugar impact, taste, and culinary performance?
narrative: The user is seeking detailed information on natural sweeteners that have a low glycemic index and are suitable for use in baked goods. Relevant documents should discuss the glycemic index, nutritional profile, and flavor characteristics of sweeteners such as yacon syrup, stevia, monk fruit, erythritol, and others, and should provide guidance on how each performs in baking (e.g., texture, browning, sweetness level). Scientific studies, dietary guidelines, or reputable culinary sources that compare these sweeteners are considered relevant, while documents focusing solely on high‑glycemic sugars or non‑culinary uses are not.

title: Offshore Wind Policy
description: What are the recent policy developments and main challenges affecting the deployment of offshore wind farms in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks up-to-date information (approximately the last five years) on U.S. offshore wind policy, including legislation, regulatory frameworks, permitting processes, incentive mechanisms, and technical or environmental challenges. Relevant documents should discuss specific statutes, agency actions, case studies, or expert analyses directly related to offshore wind development in U.S. coastal waters, rather than generic renewable‑energy topics.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the main challenges faced by developing countries when implementing renewable energy policies?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the obstacles that low‑ and middle‑income nations encounter while adopting renewable energy strategies. Relevant documents should discuss economic, regulatory, technical, and social barriers, provide examples or case studies from specific developing countries, and may include proposed solutions or recommendations. Materials focused solely on developed countries, generic renewable technology descriptions without policy context, or unrelated topics such as consumer products are not relevant.

title: Renault Tire Selection
description: What factors should I consider when choosing replacement tires for a 2002 Renault Space IV 2.0 dCi?
narrative: The user is looking for a detailed guide to selecting appropriate tires for their 2002 Renault Space IV 2.0 dCi. Relevant documents should discuss tire size specifications, load and speed ratings, seasonal vs. all‑season options, brand reliability, price‑performance trade‑offs, and any vehicle‑specific recommendations. Resources that merely list stock prices, login errors, or unrelated financial data are not relevant.

title: Solar Panel Incentives
description: What federal and state incentives are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States in 2024?
narrative: The user is looking for current (2024) information on monetary and tax incentives, rebates, and loan programs offered by the U.S. federal government and individual states for installing residential solar photovoltaic systems. Relevant documents must describe each incentive's type, eligibility criteria, application process, and the amount or percentage of the benefit, and should be specific to the United States.

title: Coral Reef Resilience
description: How are increasing sea temperatures affecting the resilience of coral reefs in the Indo‑Pacific region?
narrative: The user is looking for recent scientific information on the impact of rising sea surface temperatures on coral reefs specifically in the Indo‑Pacific. Relevant documents should include peer‑reviewed studies, long‑term monitoring data, or meta‑analyses that discuss bleaching events, physiological stress responses, adaptation or recovery mechanisms, and projected future impacts on reef health. Sources that focus on unrelated regions, non‑scientific commentary, or topics such as tourism, transportation, or car rentals are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Productivity
description: How does remote work influence employee productivity across different industries and job functions?
narrative: The user is seeking research, case studies, or statistical analyses that examine the effects of remote work on productivity. Relevant documents should provide quantitative or qualitative evidence, compare remote and onsite work, discuss factors such as collaboration tools, work‑life balance, and industry‑specific trends, and ideally cover multiple sectors or job roles.

title: Quantum Key Exchange
description: What are the current standards and best practices for implementing quantum‑resistant key exchange protocols in enterprise networks?
narrative: The user is seeking up‑to‑date technical guidance on quantum‑safe key exchange mechanisms, including accepted standards (e.g., NIST post‑quantum cryptography recommendations), algorithm choices, implementation considerations, and security trade‑offs for corporate environments. Relevant documents should discuss these protocols, provide practical deployment advice, or evaluate their performance and security; any material focused on unrelated topics such as news events, automotive safety, or historical accidents is not relevant.

title: Solar Tax Credits
description: What federal tax credits are available for residential solar panel installations in 2024?
narrative: The user wants current information on U.S. federal financial incentives for homeowners installing solar photovoltaic systems in 2024, including credit amounts, eligibility requirements, filing instructions, and deadlines. Relevant sources must specifically address the 2024 federal tax credit (e.g., the Investment Tax Credit) and provide actionable details. Documents that only discuss state/local incentives, outdated years, or unrelated renewable technologies are not relevant.

title: Hybrid Car Incentives
description: What federal and state tax credits and rebates are available for purchasing a plug‑in hybrid vehicle in the United States as of 2024?
narrative: The user is seeking current, detailed information about monetary incentives (tax credits, rebates, subsidies) for plug‑in hybrid cars, including eligibility criteria, credit amounts, application processes, and any expiration dates. Relevant documents should explicitly discuss U.S. federal programs (e.g., IRS Section 30D) and state‑level incentives that apply to plug‑in hybrids, provide numeric values, and be dated 2023‑2024 or later. Sources that only mention conventional hybrids, pure electric vehicles, or non‑U.S. programs are not relevant.

title: Carbon Pricing Impact
description: How have carbon pricing mechanisms affected electricity prices for residential consumers in Europe since 2015?
narrative: The user seeks analytical reports, academic studies, or government assessments that examine the influence of carbon taxes or emissions trading schemes on household electricity costs across European nations after 2015. Relevant material should present quantitative data, before‑and‑after comparisons, and discuss variations in policy design. Sources limited to non‑European regions or lacking empirical evidence (e.g., opinion pieces) are not relevant.

title: Renewable Energy Policies
description: What recent European governmental policies promote the adoption of renewable energy?
narrative: The user wants information on the newest (2022‑2024) laws, regulations, and incentive programs enacted by European countries to increase renewable energy generation and consumption. Relevant documents should describe the policy measures (e.g., subsidies, tax credits, feed‑in tariffs, grid‑integration mandates), their intended targets (solar, wind, bioenergy, etc.), and any reported implementation outcomes or timelines.

title: Renewable Energy Policy
description: What are the latest national policies and incentives promoting renewable energy deployment in European Union member states as of 2024?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive, up-to-date information on official government policies, legislation, and financial incentives that support the adoption of renewable energy technologies (e.g., wind, solar, biomass) across EU countries. Relevant documents should detail policy objectives, funding mechanisms, eligibility criteria, and implementation timelines, and be sourced from governmental or reputable policy‑analysis publications. Documents focusing solely on private‑sector initiatives, non‑EU countries, or historical policies before 2020 are not relevant.

title: Carbon Pricing Impact
description: How does carbon pricing affect the competitiveness of manufacturing firms in the EU?
narrative: The user is looking for scholarly or policy-oriented sources that examine the economic consequences of carbon pricing mechanisms—such as the EU Emissions Trading System or national carbon taxes—on the manufacturing sector's competitiveness. Relevant documents should provide empirical data, case studies, or model‑based analyses covering outcomes like production costs, export performance, investment decisions, or market share for manufacturers. Acceptable sources include peer‑reviewed journal articles, government or intergovernmental reports, and reputable think‑tank publications dated from 2000 onward. Materials that address carbon pricing only in environmental or climate‑change contexts without economic evaluation, or that focus exclusively on other sectors (e.g., transport, power generation), are not relevant.

title: Remote Work Productivity
description: How does remote work affect employee productivity compared to traditional office settings?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence‑based analyses that compare productivity outcomes of remote employees with those working on‑site. Relevant documents should provide quantitative data (e.g., performance metrics, output rates, KPI changes) or well‑structured qualitative assessments (e.g., case studies, expert interviews) that directly address the impact of remote work on productivity. Sources may include academic research, industry reports, white papers, or credible surveys conducted after 2019. Documents focusing solely on unrelated topics such as leisure activities, gaming, or non‑work‑related remote activities are not relevant.

title: Solar Incentives
description: What federal tax credits and state-level incentives are available for residential solar panel installations in the United States in 2024?
narrative: The user is seeking up-to-date, detailed information about financial incentives for installing solar panels on a home in the US. Relevant documents must describe the federal Investment Tax Credit (ITC) amount, eligibility criteria, filing procedures, and any state, county, or utility‑specific rebates or grants that are active in 2024. The user also wants guidance on deadlines, application forms, and any interaction between federal and state programs. Sources that only discuss general renewable energy policy, foreign programs (e.g., French ARCE), or unrelated employment assistance are not relevant.

title: Electric Vehicle Lifecycle
description: What are the environmental impacts of electric cars throughout their entire lifecycle compared to conventional gasoline vehicles?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive, data‑driven comparison of the full lifecycle environmental footprint of electric vehicles versus internal‑combustion engine cars. Relevant material must cover raw material extraction, manufacturing processes, energy sources for charging, use‑phase emissions, and end‑of‑life disposal or recycling, and provide quantitative assessments or reputable lifecycle‑assessment studies. Documents that only address a single phase, anecdotal opinions, or marketing claims are not considered relevant.

title: Maritime Lantern History
description: What types of lanterns were used on seafaring vessels during the Age of Exploration and how did they impact navigation?
narrative: The user is looking for historical information about lanterns specifically employed on ships from the 15th to 17th centuries, including their design, fuel, illumination characteristics, and their role in navigation and safety at sea. Relevant documents should discuss maritime lighting technology of that era, reference specific shipboard lantern models or practices, and explain how these lanterns influenced navigation, crew operations, or maritime culture. Sources that focus solely on modern decorative or household oil lamps, safety tips, or contemporary product reviews are not relevant.

title: Microplastic Food Webs
description: How do microplastic particles affect the transfer of contaminants through marine food webs?
narrative: The user seeks recent, peer‑reviewed scientific evidence describing the mechanisms by which microplastics influence the accumulation and biomagnification of chemical contaminants within marine trophic levels. Relevant documents should detail experimental or field studies (preferably from the last five years) that quantify microplastic ingestion, contaminant binding, and subsequent transfer to predators, and should discuss ecological implications or mitigation strategies. Sources lacking quantitative data, focusing solely on policy, or unrelated to marine systems are not relevant.

title: EU Renewable Energy Policy
description: What recent policy measures has the European Union implemented to promote renewable energy deployment?
narrative: The user seeks authoritative information on EU-level initiatives that support renewable energy, including legislation, targets, financing mechanisms, and regulatory frameworks enacted after 2015. Relevant documents should detail specific policies, their objectives, implementation timelines, and measurable impacts on renewable energy capacity or generation within the EU.

title: Offshore Wind Scaling
description: What are the main technical, regulatory, and economic challenges to scaling offshore wind power in the North Sea by 2030, and what strategies are being proposed to overcome them?
narrative: The user is seeking a comprehensive overview of the obstacles and proposed solutions for expanding offshore wind capacity in the North Sea within the next decade. Relevant documents should discuss technical issues such as turbine size, grid integration, and maintenance; regulatory frameworks including marine spatial planning and permitting; economic factors like financing models, cost reductions, and market incentives; and strategic initiatives from governments, industry consortia, and research institutions. Sources that merely mention offshore wind without addressing these specific challenges or that focus on unrelated regions or timeframes are not relevant.
